---
title: "Lecture 14: Conditional Statements & Looping"
subtitle: "{{< meta course.number >}} - {{< meta course.title >}}"
author: "{{< meta instructor.name >}}"
date: "February 17, 2026"
code-annotations: below
---


## {#title-slide data-menu-title="Title Slide" background="#053660"}

<div class="custom-title">
[Lecture 14]{.baby-blue-text}
</div>

<div class="custom-subtitle">
[Conditional Statements & Looping]{.white-text}
</div>

<div class="custom-subtitle3">
Week 4 | Unit II: JavaScript Functions & Events<br>
{{< meta course.number >}} - {{< meta course.title >}}<br>
{{< meta instructor.name >}}, {{< meta instructor.title >}}
</div>

---

## {data-menu-title="Learning Objectives"}

[Learning Objectives]{.slide-title}

<hr>

::: {.incremental .body-text-m}
- Make decisions with `if` and `switch`
- Repeat tasks using loops
- Compare `for`, `while`, and `do...while`
- Use `break` and `continue` correctly
- Avoid common infinite-loop mistakes
:::

---

## {data-menu-title="Conditionals"}

[If / Else Logic]{.slide-title}

<hr>

```{.javascript code-line-numbers="|1|2|3|4|5|6|7"}
const marks = 72;

if (marks >= 90) {
  console.log("Grade A");
} else if (marks >= 60) {
  console.log("Grade B");
} else {
  console.log("Grade C");
}
```

---

## {data-menu-title="Comparison Ops"}

[Comparison and Logical Operators]{.slide-title2}

<hr>

```javascript
age >= 18
score === 100
userRole === "admin" && isActive
isGuest || hasAccess
```

- Use `===` for strict checks
- Combine conditions with `&&` and `||`

---

## {data-menu-title="Switch"}

[Switch Statement]{.slide-title}

<hr>

```{.javascript code-line-numbers="|1|2-3|4-6|7-9|10-11"}
const day = "Friday";

switch (day) {
  case "Monday":
    console.log("Start of week");
    break;
  case "Friday":
    console.log("Almost weekend");
    break;
  default:
    console.log("Regular day");
}
```

---

## {data-menu-title="For Loop"}

[For Loop]{.slide-title}

<hr>

```{.javascript code-line-numbers="|1|2|3"}
for (let i = 1; i <= 5; i++) {
  console.log("Iteration:", i);
}
```

Best when iteration count is known.

---

## {data-menu-title="While Loops"}

[While and Do...While]{.slide-title2}

<hr>

```javascript
let count = 0;
while (count < 3) {
  console.log(count);
  count++;
}
```

```javascript
let n = 0;
do {
  console.log(n);
  n++;
} while (n < 3);
```

- `while`: condition first
- `do...while`: runs at least once

---

## {data-menu-title="For Of In"}

[for...of vs for...in]{.slide-title}

<hr>

```javascript
const nums = [10, 20, 30];
for (const value of nums) {
  console.log(value);
}

const user = { name: "Aman", age: 21 };
for (const key in user) {
  console.log(key, user[key]);
}
```

---

## {data-menu-title="Break Continue"}

[break and continue]{.slide-title}

<hr>

```javascript
for (let i = 1; i <= 10; i++) {
  if (i === 4) continue;
  if (i === 8) break;
  console.log(i);
}
```

- `continue`: skip current iteration
- `break`: exit loop completely

---

## {data-menu-title="Pitfalls"}

[Common Loop Pitfalls]{.slide-title}

<hr>

::: {.incremental .body-text-m}
- Forgetting to update loop variable
- Wrong boundary (`<=` vs `<`)
- Modifying array while iterating carelessly
- Nesting too many loops unnecessarily
:::

---

## {#end-slide data-menu-title="End Slide" background="#053660"}

<div class="page-center">
<div class="custom-subtitle">
[Questions?]{.white-text}
</div>
<div class="custom-subtitle3">
Next: Lecture 15 - Objects & Scope
</div>
</div>
